0:["rs_rePhfydXe1AYX_q-va",[[["",{"children":["about",{"children":["__PAGE__",{}]}]},"$undefined","$undefined",true],["",{"children":["about",{"children":["__PAGE__",{},[["$L1",["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-12","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs font-mono text-brand-gold uppercase tracking-widest mb-2","children":"Framework"}],["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-3xl md:text-4xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"About"}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"The Theoretical Foundation"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed space-y-3","children":[["$","p",null,{"children":["The Telecom Economy Continuum emerged from a straightforward observation: the telecom industry has extensive standards for network architecture (3GPP), operations (TMF), and measurement (ETSI, GSMA), but no coherent framework that explains ",["$","em",null,{"children":"why"}]," certain observability functions are economically necessary — not just technically desirable."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The six laws are derived from first principles of network economics: the relationship between capacity, demand, revenue stratification, technology transitions, competitive markets, and information asymmetry. They are not normative recommendations. They are structural conditions that exist regardless of operator strategy or vendor choice."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The ten observability functions follow deductively from the laws. If a law holds — and the evidence suggests all six do — then certain measurement functions are theoretically necessary. An operator that lacks them is not making a strategic choice; it is operating with incomplete information about its own economic position."}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"Distinction from Existing Frameworks"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-3","children":[["$","div","TMF (TM Forum)",{"className":"rounded-xl border border-[var(--card-border)] bg-[var(--card)] p-4","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm font-sans font-semibold text-[var(--foreground)] mb-1","children":"TMF (TM Forum)"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed","children":"TMF provides operational process frameworks (eTOM, TAM). The Continuum provides economic grounding for why certain measurement capabilities are non-optional. They are complementary, not competing."}]]}],["$","div","ETSI / 3GPP",{"className":"rounded-xl border border-[var(--card-border)] bg-[var(--card)] p-4","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm font-sans font-semibold text-[var(--foreground)] mb-1","children":"ETSI / 3GPP"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed","children":"Technical standards define what the network can do. The Continuum explains what must be observed given what the network does. The independence principle — that observation must come from outside the measured system — is absent from technical standards."}]]}],["$","div","GSMA",{"className":"rounded-xl border border-[var(--card-border)] bg-[var(--card)] p-4","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm font-sans font-semibold text-[var(--foreground)] mb-1","children":"GSMA"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed","children":"GSMA guidelines are industry consensus documents. The Continuum makes falsifiable theoretical claims: if Law II holds, then F2 is necessary. If that prediction is wrong, the framework should be revised."}]]}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"The Author"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"rounded-xl border border-[var(--card-border)] bg-[var(--card)] p-6","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-start gap-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"w-14 h-14 rounded-full bg-[var(--muted-bg)] border border-[var(--card-border)] flex items-center justify-center shrink-0","children":["$","span",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--muted)]","children":"V"}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex-1","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"text-base font-sans font-semibold text-[var(--foreground)] mb-1","children":"Vugar Aliyev"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-[var(--muted)] mb-3","children":"Telecom Strategy & Network Observability"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Telecom industry professional with experience across MNO strategy, network quality management, and the commercial application of observability tools. The Continuum framework synthesises that experience into a theoretical model intended to be useful to operators, vendors, and consultants working on network quality economics."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex gap-3","children":[["$","a",null,{"href":"https://www.linkedin.com/in/-vugaraliyev/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-xs text-brand-blue hover:underline","children":[["$","$L2",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M15 3h6v6","key":"1q9fwt"}],["path",{"d":"M10 14 21 3","key":"gplh6r"}],["path",{"d":"M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6","key":"a6xqqp"}]],"className":"lucide-external-link w-3.5 h-3.5"}]," LinkedIn"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"mailto:boss@vaitechpro.com","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-xs text-[var(--muted)] hover:text-[var(--foreground)]","children":[["$","$L2",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m22 7-8.991 5.727a2 2 0 0 1-2.009 0L2 7","key":"132q7q"}],["rect",{"x":"2","y":"4","width":"20","height":"16","rx":"2","key":"izxlao"}]],"className":"lucide-mail w-3.5 h-3.5"}]," boss@vaitechpro.com"]}]]}]]}]]}]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"How to Cite This Work"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"rounded-xl border border-[var(--card-border)] bg-[var(--card)] p-4","children":["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs font-mono text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed","children":["Aliyev, V. (",2026,"). ",["$","em",null,{"children":"Telecom Economy Continuum: The Laws of Network Observability"}],". Retrieved from telecomcontinuum.com"]}]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mb-10","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-serif text-[var(--foreground)] mb-4","children":"Contribute"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-[var(--muted)] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Use cases and tool mappings can be contributed via GitHub. Each use case is a JSON file with a defined schema. The author reviews and publishes contributions that meet the quality standard — grounded in the framework, with a quantified economic outcome."}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/use-cases","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 border border-[var(--card-border)] text-[var(--foreground)] px-4 py-2 rounded-lg text-sm font-sans
